project_summary: relay-dev is a PowerShell-based phase-driven AI development runner. This run is a small browser-only system build intended to exercise task-lane parallelization through disjoint example files.
constraints: Build a small browser-only maintenance request board under examples/parallel_smoke_system/.
Use only HTML, CSS, vanilla JavaScript, and PowerShell; do not add package managers, bundlers, servers, databases, external images, or CDNs.
Do not modify existing relay-dev core areas such as app/, config/, tests/regression.ps1, or docs/guide/ as part of the relay-dev task implementation.
Keep implementation changes scoped to new files under examples/parallel_smoke_system/ unless a later phase explains a necessary exception.
Do not assign the same changed file to multiple Phase4 tasks.
The UI should be quiet, readable, operations-tool-like, and responsive without relying on DESIGN.md.
Follow the current human_review setting in config/settings.yaml; do not change it as part of the relay-dev task implementation.
risks: If Phase4 groups all example files into one task, the run will not exercise parallel task lanes well.
If two tasks edit the same file, the smoke test may become a workspace-conflict test instead of a parallel scheduling test.
Static verification cannot fully replace a quick browser check of the generated index.html.
The app.js and storage.js interface must be clearly defined so dependent UI work does not drift.
open_questions: No task requirement is unresolved; during execution, observe run-state, events, and jobs to confirm how parallel leases, jobs, and commits are recorded.
visual_constraints: Use a quiet operations-tool layout rather than a marketing page.
Show request status and priority clearly with compact labels.
Keep the interface readable on mobile and desktop.
Do not use external images, icon CDNs, or generated visual assets.
